label: hunter | live | wife
sentence: A well-known hunter lived far up North with his beautiful wife and two young sons.

label: he | leave | lodge
sentence: Every day, he left their remote lodge to hunt the animals that were their main source of food.

label: game | be | ∅
sentence: Back then, game was plentiful, and his hard work in the hunt always brought good results.

label: they | see | anyone
sentence: Because they were so isolated, they rarely saw anyone outside their family.

label: they | play | lodge
sentence: The two boys were still too young to join their father in the hunt, so they played near the lodge.

label: they | start | ∅
sentence: While they were busy, they started noticing a young man visiting their lodge whenever their father was away.

label: visit | happen | ∅
sentence: These visits happened again and again.

label: boy | ask | mother
sentence: One day, the older boy asked his mother, "Mother, who is that tall young man who keeps coming here when father is gone?

sentence: Does he want to see father?

"Naubesah, you foolish child," their mother scolded.

sentence: It’s not manly to hang around the lodge all day.

label: you | become | warrior
sentence: You won’t become a warrior by reporting every little thing you see and hear to your father.

label: ∅ | keep | ∅
sentence: Keep quiet about it.

sentence: The boys listened, but as they grew older and kept seeing the stranger, they decided to talk to their mother again.

sentence: This time, they said they wanted to tell their father everything they’d witnessed.

label: man | use | path
sentence: The young man didn’t use the path or carry food, so if he had a message for their father, why didn’t he deliver it directly?

label: they | know | ∅
sentence: They knew that important messages were always given to men, never women.

label: mother | become | ∅
sentence: Their mother became very angry.

label: she | threaten | ∅
sentence: "If you tell anyone, I’ll kill you," she threatened.

label: fear | silence | they
sentence: Fear silenced them for a while, but they still noticed the stranger’s secret visits.

label: they | decide | ∅
sentence: Finally, they decided to tell their father.

label: they | tell | everything
sentence: One day, while they were learning to follow animal tracks in the woods, they told him everything.

label: face | darken | ∅
sentence: The father’s face darkened.

label: he | look | silence
sentence: After a long silence, he looked up and said, "It is done.

label: boy | stay | sunset
sentence: You boys stay here until sunset, then come back to the lodge and you will find me."

label: he | walk | ∅
sentence: He walked away slowly, and the boys played until it was time to return.

label: mother | be | ∅
sentence: When they got back, their mother was gone.

label: they | dare | ∅
sentence: They didn’t dare ask where she had gone, and from that day on, her name was never mentioned again in the lodge.

label: year | pass | ∅
sentence: Years passed, and the boys became men.

label: she | linger | lodge
sentence: Though their mother was never seen again managing the home, walking the forest paths, or near the river, she lingered near the lodge, transformed but still there.

label: arm | wither | ∅
sentence: She appeared to her sons at twilight after their hunts—her face ghastly, her arms withered.

label: she | open | door
sentence: At night, she would silently open the lodge door and lean over them as they slept.

label: they | see | pale
sentence: Often, they saw only her pale, bony forehead floating in the air, mocking them along wild forest trails or in the dark of night.

label: she | terrify | they
sentence: She terrified them all, turning every place she appeared into something dreadful.

label: father | agree | suffering
sentence: After long suffering, the father and his sons agreed to leave their homeland.

label: they | journey | ∅
sentence: They journeyed south, traveling many days along a great lake’s shore.

label: they | round | bluff
sentence: One day, they rounded a rocky bluff and came upon a waterfall where a river flowed from the lake.

label: they | hear | noise
sentence: As they admired the rushing water, they heard a heavy rolling noise behind them.

label: they | see | ∅
sentence: Turning, they saw a woman’s skull rolling along the beach, chasing them fast.

label: body | appear | wood
sentence: Then, from the nearby woods, a headless body appeared, running swiftly toward the shore.

label: skull | pursue | it
sentence: The skull too pursued it, and soon they joined together, rushing ahead to catch the hunter and his sons.

label: man | know | ∅
sentence: Terrified, the men didn’t know how to escape.

label: one | spot | crane
sentence: At that moment, one of the sons spotted a large, stately crane perched on a rock in the rapids.

label: they | cry | ∅
sentence: They cried out, "Grandfather, help us!

sentence: Carry us across the falls so we can get away from her.

The crane, old and wise, had been deep in thought alone amid the swirling waters.

label: he | warn | they
sentence: "Be careful,” he warned them.

The old bird came to her and repeated his warning about not touching the crown of his head several times, making sure she understood.

label: she | promise | ∅
sentence: She promised to obey.

label: she | try | ∅
sentence: But as soon as they were flying over the river, she tried to break her promise.

sentence: "There," he said as she sank beneath the waves, "you have always done what was forbidden.

sentence: The woman disappeared, swept far away by the rushing waters, lost alone in the wide, endless wilderness.

label: family | take | bird
sentence: Grateful for the crane’s help, the hunter’s family took the bird as their symbol, and under the protection of the Crane that Crossed the River, they prospered with plenty by day and peace by night.
